conbody.p: This section describes the new features in the Juniper Cloud-Native Router 23.1 release.

note.p: Juniper Cloud-Native Router 23.1 release supports only L2 deployments. L3 deployments are not supported for this release.

section.title: New Features in Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 23.1

li.p: Support for native VLAN—Juniper Cloud-Native Router now supports receiving and forwarding bridged Ethernet frames with 802.1Q VLAN tags. Typically, trunk ports accept only tagged packets, and the untagged packets are dropped. You can enable a JCNR fabric trunk port to accept untagged packets by configuring a native VLAN identifier (ID) on the interface on which you want the untagged packets to be received. When a JCNR fabric trunk port is enabled to accept untagged packets, such packets are forwarded with the native VLAN ID applied to them.

Support for preventing local switching—Juniper Cloud-Native Router now provides support to prevent interfaces in a bridge domain that are a part of the same VLAN group, from transmitting ethernet frame copies in between those interfaces. The noLocalSwitching: key provides the option to enable the functionality on the selected VLAN IDs.

note.p: The noLocalSwitching: functionality is a Technology Preview feature in the Juniper Cloud-Native Router Release 23.1.
